_comment: THE THOUSAND-KNOT BAZAAR — a sprawling fantasy market wound around the dry bed of the Sleeping River. The old stories say the river did not dry up: it was SOLD, and the city that grew in the empty channel is the slow repayment of that debt. Reached from the Vale through the sun-warmed archway. Two design pillars govern the layout: (1) NAVIGATION — the Vein (a sunken dry channel) runs the length of the world and everything drains toward it ('follow the channel and you'll never be lost'); five tall VANES (one per district) are the skyline compass; KNUCKLE plazas are rest/anchor points. (2) ATMOSPHERE & LORE — each district (a KNOT) has its own palette (a region tint), its own Vane, and one fragment of the central story, surfaced by Examining its lore signpost or a Knuckle notice-board. Coordinates are world-space pixels; +y is south, -y is north; colors are [r,g,b] 0..1. Same schema as vale.json: world_art draws by 'type', world_controller uses 'interactables' for proximity, 'portals' for travel, 'npc_companion' for the merchant's stationary partner. New here: 'vein' (the dry channel band) and the prop types chimney/prism_tower/ivory_spire/crooked_mast/sky_anchor (the Vanes), dry_well/notice_board/shrine (Knuckle furniture), wanderer (the wet-boots man), crate. A prop's optional 'lore' string is read out when Examined.
_regions_comment: The eight named areas: the central Vein, the two Knuckle plazas, and the five Knots, each with a 'tint' that washes its palette underfoot so a glance tells you which district you're in. Region ids are world-namespaced, so the companion REMEMBERS each Knot it has wandered into.
_atmosphere_comment: A warm, dusty wash over the whole bazaar — a golden light, drifting dust motes, a gentle lantern breathing. Per-district mood comes from the region tints above, not from a separate per-area lerp.
_vein_comment: THE VEIN — the sunken dry riverbed that runs the full width of the world through its middle. The navigation spine: every district's paths drain to it, both Knuckles sit on it, and the entry archway is just off it. Drawn as a dusty channel with sunken banks, an old high-water rim, cracked mud, and a faint dust 'current'.
_paths_comment: The artery network: a spine running the length of the Vein, with a feeder from every Knot down into it, and a climb north from the North Knuckle up to the High Stalls. This is the readable skeleton beneath the maze of stalls.
_interactables_comment: Everything you can walk up to and Examine. The five VANES are the skyline compass (tall, each tinted to its Knot). The KNUCKLES carry a dry well, a notice-board (the central story + bounties), and a rest shrine. Each Knot has a lore SIGNPOST that reads out its story fragment. The color merchant (a 'shopkeeper': Examining opens the colour shop) and their bonded companion keep the onboarding Spice Knot. The wet-boots WANDERER haunts the Ragpicker's Tangle. Stalls are solid, so dense rows naturally form walkable mazes — tidy in Spice (onboarding), tangled in the Ragpicker's. Keep ids unique and stable.

interactables.label: the Copper Chimney
interactables.lore: The Copper Chimney, forever smoking over the Spice Knot. Lost? Find its smoke and you've found your way.

interactables.label: the Prism Tower
interactables.lore: The Prism Tower throws rainbows over the Glassblowers' Run — a colour you can steer by from half the bazaar.

interactables.label: the Ivory Finger
interactables.lore: The Ivory Finger, a carved spire pointing skyward over the hushed Bonewrights' Knot.

interactables.label: the Crooked Mast
interactables.lore: The Crooked Mast leans over the Ragpicker's Tangle like a ship run aground in a river that left without it.

interactables.label: the Sky-Anchor
interactables.lore: The Sky-Anchor hangs over the High Stalls on its chains — visible clear across the channel, for an anchor with no water to drop into.

interactables.label: the dry fountain
interactables.lore: A dry fountain. There's a green stain a hand's-width above the floor — the old waterline. The river stood this high, once.

interactables.label: the South Knuckle notice-board
interactables.lore: THE THOUSAND-KNOT BAZAAR, in the bed of the Sleeping River. The river didn't dry up — it was sold, in a bargain no one quite remembers. The Knots are the slow repayment of that debt.

interactables.label: a roadside shrine
interactables.lore: A little shrine with a steady candle. A good place to stop a while, here at the bazaar's main crossing.

interactables.label: the dry fountain
interactables.lore: Another dry fountain, another high-water mark. Some vendors keep them swept, as if expecting the water back any morning now.

interactables.label: the North Knuckle notice-board
interactables.lore: All roads run downhill to the Vein — follow the dry channel and you'll never be lost. They say the water returns when the debt is paid. A few quiet vendors are betting on when.

interactables.label: a roadside shrine
interactables.lore: A candle gutters in a stone niche. From here the climb north leads up to the High Stalls and the long views.

interactables.label: a painted signboard
interactables.lore: The first stalls were boats. Look at the curve of those old roofs — they were hulls, beached in the dry channel and never refloated.

interactables.label: an etched glass placard
interactables.lore: We melt sand from the dry bed. Every window in the Run is a piece of the river, made to hold light instead of water.

interactables.label: a carved bone marker
interactables.lore: The Bonewrights keep the founder's ledgers. The page with the name is missing. It was removed, not lost.

interactables.label: a tin scrap-sign
interactables.lore: Someone down here sells river-charts. Charts of a river that doesn't exist yet. Ask for the man with wet boots.

interactables.label: a weathered vista-sign
interactables.lore: From up here you see the whole channel. On still mornings the dust in it moves like a current. The ones who watch are the ones who bet.

interactables.label: a man with wet boots
interactables.lore: His boots are wet — in a city built on a dry river, that means something. He deals in charts of water not yet returned, and asks only one thing: when?

_npc_companion_comment: The color merchant's bonded companion, kept beside them in the Spice Knot — a stationary puppet (no brain, never moves). 'look' is its resting-look (the same float bundle a friend's companion travels with): perked ears, a lively idle, a warm coat, a touch larger.
_portals_comment: The way home: the sun-warmed archway sits just off the South Knuckle (the hub). Walk into it to return to the Vale, stepping out beside the arch you came through (vale_bazaar_portal). Its id MUST stay 'bazaar_entry' (the Vale's portal targets it).
